sections.paragraph:
an unused root [referred to מוּן in Thes.], Arab. مَانَ to lie, to speak falsehood, Eth. ተመየነ፡ to be crafty, cunning, Heb. prob. to bear an appearance, to pretend, whence תְּמוּנָה appearance, form, and-

מין

m. form, hence species, kind, sort, comp. Gr. ἰδέα, which also denotes both form and kind. Always in the phrase לְמִינֹו, לְמִינֵהוּ “according to its kind,” Gen 1:11, 12 , 21 , 25 Lev 11:15, 16 Lev 11:16 לְמִינָהּ Gen 1:24, 25 pl. לְמִינֵיהֶם Gen 1:21. (Syr. ܡܺܝܢܐܳ family, race.)
